```{r}
# plot(mult_jlcmm_m2, outcome = 1, which = "fit")
```

## TO DO

External validation: what do these classes look like? Do they just represent different genotypes? How do we check this and are they truly valid LATENT classes?

# Comparing models using time-dependent AUC

*For the Cox PH models = using timeROC, from the package timeROC*

*For the joint models = using tvAUC, from the package JMbayes2*

## Defining evaluation time points for time-dependent AUC comparison

```{r}
# We define evaluation time points for time-dependent AUC comparison
times <- c(2, 3, 4, 5, 6)
```

## Time-dependent AUC for fitted Cox PH models

```{r}
# We define a function to compute time-dependent AUC for Cox models using timeROC
get_cox_auc <- function(model, times) {
  
  T <- surv_data_out$time_to_event
  delta <- surv_data_out$event
  
  lp <- predict(model, newdata = surv_data_out, type = "lp")
  
  timeROC::timeROC(
    T = T,
    delta = delta,
    marker = lp,
    cause = 1,
    times = times,
    iid = TRUE
  )$AUC
}
```

```{r}
# We compute time-dependent AUC for Cox model with p-tau181
auc_ptau_bl <- get_cox_auc(surv_model_ptau_bl_final, times)
 
# # We compute time-dependent AUC for Cox model with NfL
auc_nfl_bl <- get_cox_auc(surv_model_nfl_bl_final, times)

# We compute time-dependent AUC for Cox model with both p-tau181 and NfL 
auc_both_bl <- get_cox_auc(surv_model_both_bl_final, times)
```

## Time-dependent AUC for fitted joint models

*From: <https://cloud.r-project.org/web/packages/JMbayes2/JMbayes2.pdf>*

*The function tvAUC requires the following:*

- *"newdata = a data.frame that contains the longitudinal and covariate information for the subjects for which prediction of survival probabilities is required. The names of the variables in this data.frame must be the same as in the data.frames that were used to fit the linear mixed effects and the event process model that were supplied as the two first argument of jm."*

```{r}
# We construct prediction datasets for the joint models by merging the  longitudinal and survival data
newdata <- dplyr::left_join(
  long_data_out,
  dplyr::select(surv_data_out, RID, age_at_baseline, time_to_event, event),
  by = "RID"
)
```

```{r}
# We define a function to compute time-dependent AUC for joint models using tvAUC
get_jm_auc <- function(model, times) {
  
  aucs <- sapply(times, function(t) {
  
    tmp <- tvAUC(
      model,
      newdata = newdata,
      Tstart = 1,
      Thoriz = t
    )
    
    tmp$auc
  })
  
  return(as.numeric(aucs))
}
```

```{r}
# We compute time-dependent AUC for the joint model including p-tau181 
auc_ptau_jm <- get_jm_auc(joint_model_ptau, times)

# We compute time-dependent AUC for the joint model including NfL 
auc_nfl_jm <- get_jm_auc(joint_model_nfl, times)

# We compute time-dependent AUC for the multivariate joint model 
auc_multi_jm <- get_jm_auc(multi_joint_model, times)

```

## Time-dependent AUC for latent class joint models

#### Function for getting predictions from multivariate joint latent class model

```{r}
# Defining a function to predict cumulative hazard (1 - S(t)) for multivariate joint latent class models (these cannot be passed to dynpred in the lcmm package)
predict_mjlcmm <- function(model, data, ID, landmark, times) {
  
#   Extracting the unique ID's
  ids <- unique(data[[ID]])
#   Extracting number of individuals to make predictions for
  N <- length(unique(data[[ID]]))
#   Extracting the nuber of classes
  G <- model$ng
#   Number of horizons
  n_h <- length(times)
  print(n_h)
  
#   Extracting the posterior probabilities (given a two class model)
  post_prob <- model$pprob[, 3:4]
  
#   Extracting the Weibull hazard parameters from the model object 
  scale <- sapply(1:G, function(g) {
                   exp(model$best[paste0("event1 +/-sqrt(Weibull2) class ", as.character(g))])})
  shape <- sapply(1:G, function(g) {
                   exp(model$best[paste0("event1 +/-sqrt(Weibull1) class ", as.character(g))])})
  
#   Initiating a matrix to save the predictions for each individual at each horizon
  S_pred <- matrix(0, nrow = N, ncol = n_h)
  
#   Saving the class-specific survivals at the landmark time
  S_land <- sapply(1:G, function(g)
    exp(-(landmark / scale[g])^shape[g]))
  
#   Looping over all indiivudals
  for(i in 1:N) {
    
#     GEtting the posterior probabilities for each individual
    pp_i <- post_prob[i, ]
    
#     Looping over the horizons
    for(j in 1:n_h) {
      t <- times[j]
    
#      Getting the conditional class-specific survivals
      Sg <- sapply(1:G, function(g)
        exp(-((t) / scale[g])^shape[g]) / S_land[g])

#      Calculating the cumulative hazard (1 - survival) for inidivudal i based on their posterior probabilities and the class-specific survival functions
      S_pred[i, j] <- 1 - sum(pp_i * Sg)
    }
  }  
#   Convert to long format matching dynpred() output structure
  S_pred <- as.data.frame(S_pred)
  colnames(S_pred) <- as.character(times)
  S_pred[[ID]] <- ids

  S_pred_long <- S_pred %>%
    pivot_longer(
      cols = -all_of(ID),
      names_to  = "horizon",
      values_to = "pred"
    ) %>%
    mutate(horizon = as.numeric(horizon))
  
  names(S_pred_long) <- c("RID", "horizon", "pred")

  return(S_pred_long)
  }
```

#### Function for extracting time-dependent AUC

1.  Confidence intervals

2.  Overall model discrimination

3.  Class specific discrimination

```{r}
# Defining a function to compute time dependent AUC for joint latent class models
jlcmm_timeAUC <- function(model, newdata, ID, time_var, surv_time_var, event_var,
                          landmark, times, cause = 1) {
  
#   Initiating a data frame to save the results in at each horizon time
  output <- data.frame(
    times = times,
    cases = NA,
    AUC = NA
  )

#   Calculating the survival predictions previously defined function
  if(class(model) == "mpjlcmm") {
    dfpred <- predict_mjlcmm(
      model = model,
      data = newdata,
      ID = ID,
      landmark = landmark,
      times = times
      )
  }
  
#   Calculating the dynamic survival predictions from joint models using dynpred() in the lcmm package
  if(class(model) == "Jointlcmm") {
    dfpred <- dynpred(
      model = model,
      newdata = newdata,
      var.time = time_var,
      horizon = times,
      landmark = landmark
      )$pred
  }
  
#   Fetching the true events for each ID for each time point
  dftrue <- unique(newdata[c(ID, surv_time_var, event_var)])
  names(dftrue) <- c(ID, "time", "event")
  
#   Merging the true and predicted data frames
  merged <- merge(dfpred, dftrue, by = ID)
  
#   Looping through the horizon times
  for(i in 1:length(times)) {
    h = times[i]
    
#     Filtering for the predictions from horizon h
    data_h <- merged %>% filter(horizon == h)
    
    #     Identifiying individuals that convert (cases) in the time between landmark and landmark + horizon (l + h) or remain disease free past l + h (controls)
    cases <- data_h$event == 1 & data_h$time > landmark & data_h$time <= h
    controls <- data_h$time > h
    data_h <- data_h[cases | controls, ]
    
    roc <- timeROC(
      T = data_h$time,
      delta = data_h$event,
      marker = data_h$pred,
      cause = cause,
      times = h,
      iid = TRUE)
    
    output$AUC[i] <- roc$AUC[2]
#     Checking the events in the time window between l and l + h
    output$cases[i] <- sum(cases)
    
  }
  return(output)
}
```

```{r}
# Computing AUC for 2 class joint latent class mixed model for NfL
auc_jlcmm_m1_nfl <- jlcmm_timeAUC(jlcmm_m1_nfl, newdata,
                                  "RID", "time_years", "time_to_event", "event",
                                  1, times)

# Computing AUC for 2 class joint latent class mixed model for ptau
auc_jlcmm_m1_ptau <- jlcmm_timeAUC(jlcmm_m1_ptau, newdata,
                                   "RID", "time_years", "time_to_event", "event",
                                   1, times)

# Computing AUC for 3 class joint latent class mixed model for ptau
auc_jlcmm_m2_ptau <- jlcmm_timeAUC(jlcmm_m2_ptau, newdata,
                                   "RID", "time_years", "time_to_event", "event",
                                   1, times)

# ON IN-SAMPLE DATA
# Computing AUC for 2 class multivariate joint latent class mixed model for ptau & NfL
auc_mult_jlcmm_m1 <- jlcmm_timeAUC(mult_jlcmm_m1, jlcmm_data,
                                   "RID", "time_years", "time_to_event", "event",
                                   1, times)
```

## Table of the results

```{r}
# Time-dependent AUC for Cox and joint models summarized in a table
results_table_AUC <- data.frame(
  Model = c("Cox ptau-181", "Cox NfL", "Cox both",
            "JM ptau-181", "JM NfL", "JM both", "JLCM ptau-181", "JLCM NfL"),
  
  AUC_2y = c(auc_ptau_bl[1],
             auc_nfl_bl[1],
             auc_both_bl[1],
             auc_ptau_jm[1],
             auc_nfl_jm[1],
             auc_multi_jm[1],
             auc_jlcmm_m2_ptau[1,3],
             auc_jlcmm_m1_nfl[1,3]),
  
  AUC_3y = c(auc_ptau_bl[2],
             auc_nfl_bl[2],
             auc_both_bl[2],
             auc_ptau_jm[2],
             auc_nfl_jm[2],
             auc_multi_jm[2],
             auc_jlcmm_m2_ptau[2,3],
             auc_jlcmm_m1_nfl[2,3]),
  
  AUC_4y = c(auc_ptau_bl[3],
             auc_nfl_bl[3],
             auc_both_bl[3],
             auc_ptau_jm[3],
             auc_nfl_jm[3],
             auc_multi_jm[3],
             auc_jlcmm_m2_ptau[3,3],
             auc_jlcmm_m1_nfl[3,3]),
  
  AUC_5y = c(auc_ptau_bl[4],
             auc_nfl_bl[4],
             auc_both_bl[4],
             auc_ptau_jm[4],
             auc_nfl_jm[4],
             auc_multi_jm[4],
             auc_jlcmm_m2_ptau[4,3],
             auc_jlcmm_m1_nfl[4,3]),
  
  AUC_6y = c(auc_ptau_bl[5],
             auc_nfl_bl[5],
             auc_both_bl[5],
             auc_ptau_jm[5],
             auc_nfl_jm[5],
             auc_multi_jm[5],
             auc_jlcmm_m2_ptau[5,3],
             auc_jlcmm_m1_nfl[5,3])
)

results_table_AUC[, -1] <- round(results_table_AUC[, -1], 3)
results_table_AUC
```

```{r}
# Saving the AUC results table
saveRDS(results_table_AUC, "tables/results_table_AUC.rds")
```

## Plot of the results

```{r}
# We reshape all AUC results into long format for plotting
plot_data <- data.frame(
  Time = rep(times, 8),
  Model = rep(c("Cox ptau-181", "Cox NfL", "Cox both",
                "JM ptau-181", "JM NfL", "JM multi", "JLCM ptau-181", "JLCM NfL"),
              each = length(times)),
  AUC = c(
    auc_ptau_bl,
    auc_nfl_bl,
    auc_both_bl,
    auc_ptau_jm,
    auc_nfl_jm,
    auc_multi_jm,
    auc_jlcmm_m2_ptau$AUC,
    auc_jlcmm_m1_nfl$AUC
  )
) 

# Plot of Cox models
ggplot(plot_data %>% filter(grepl("Cox", Model)),
       aes(x = Time, y = AUC, color = Model)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.5) +
  geom_point(aes(shape = Model), size = 2) +
  ylim(0, 1) +
  labs(
    title = "Time-dependent AUC comparison",
    x = "Time (years)",
    y = "AUC"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

# Plot of joint models
ggplot(plot_data %>% filter(grepl("J", Model)),
       aes(x = Time, y = AUC, color = Model)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.5) +
  geom_point(aes(shape = Model), size = 2) +
  ylim(0, 1) +
  labs(
    title = "Time-dependent AUC comparison",
    x = "Time (years)",
    y = "AUC"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r}
png("figures/auc_plot_cox.png", width = 400, height = 300)
ggplot(plot_data %>% filter(grepl("Cox", Model)),
       aes(x = Time, y = AUC, color = Model)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.5) +
  geom_point(aes(shape = Model), size = 2) +
  ylim(0, 1) +
  labs(
    title = "Time-dependent AUC Comparison for Cox Models",
    x = "Time (years)",
    y = "AUC"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
dev.off()

png("figures/auc_plot_jm.png", width = 400, height = 300)
ggplot(plot_data %>% filter(grepl("J", Model)),
       aes(x = Time, y = AUC, color = Model)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.5) +
  geom_point(aes(shape = Model), size = 2) +
  ylim(0, 1) +
  labs(
    title = "Time-dependent AUC Comparison for Joint Models",
    x = "Time (years)",
    y = "AUC"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
dev.off()
```

# Comparing models using time-depending Brier score

## Time-dependent Brier score for fitted Cox PH models

```{r}
# We load required library
 library(pec)
 
# We specify Cox models for comparison
cox_models <- list(
   "Cox p-tau" = surv_model_ptau_bl_final,
   "Cox NfL" = surv_model_nfl_bl_final,
   "Cox both" = surv_model_both_bl_final
 )
 
# We compute Brier score using pec for Cox models
brier_cox <- pec(
   object = cox_models,
   formula = Surv(time_to_event, event) ~ 
     age_at_baseline + PTGENDER + APOE4_status,
   data = surv_data_out,
   times = times,
   cens.model = "cox"
 )
 
cox_brier <- lapply(brier_cox$AppErr, function(x)
   approx(brier_cox$time, x, xout = times)$y
 )
```

## Time-dependent Brier score for fitted joint models

```{r}
# We define a function to compute time-dependent Brier score for joint models using tvBrier
get_jm_brier <- function(model, times) {
  
  briers <- sapply(times, function(t) {
    
    tmp <- tvBrier(
      model,
      newdata = newdata,
      Tstart = 0,
      Thoriz = t
    )
    
    return(tmp$Brier)
  })
  
  return(as.numeric(briers))
}
```

```{r}
# We compute time-dependent Brier score for the joint model including p-tau181 
brier_ptau_jm <- get_jm_brier(joint_model_ptau, times)
brier_ptau_jm

# We compute time-dependent Brier score for the joint model including NfL 
brier_nfl_jm <- get_jm_brier(joint_model_nfl, times)
brier_nfl_jm

# We compute time-dependent Brier score for the multivariate joint model 
brier_multi_jm <- get_jm_brier(multi_joint_model, times)
brier_multi_jm
```

## Time-dependent Brier score for latent class joint models

```{r}
brier_jlcmm <- function(model, data, ID, time_var, surv_time_var, event_var, times, landmark) {
  
#   Initiating a data frame to save the results in at each horizon time
  output <- data.frame(
    times = times,
    Brier = NA,
    IBS = NA
  )
  
#   Calculating the survival predictions previously defined function
  if(class(model) == "mpjlcmm") {
    dfpred <- predict_mjlcmm
    (
      model = model,
      data = data,
      ID = ID,
      landmark = landmark,
      times = times
      )
  }
  
#   Calculating the dynamic survival predictions from joint models using dynpred() in the lcmm package
  if(class(model) == "Jointlcmm") {
    dfpred <- dynpred(
      model = model,
      newdata = newdata,
      var.time = time_var,
      horizon = times,
      landmark = landmark
      )$pred
  }
  
#   Fetching the true event times and events 
  dftrue <- unique(data[c(ID, surv_time_var, event_var)])
  names(dftrue) <- c(ID, "time", "event")
  
#   Merging prediction df with true df
  merged <- merge(dfpred, dftrue, by = ID)
  
#   Saving censoring indicator (1 - event indicator), times remain the same as event/censured times from the event indicator
  merged$cens_event <- 1 - merged$event
  
#  Estimting the censoring distribution with the Kaplan-Meier estimate
  km_cens <- survfit(Surv(merged$time, merged$cens_event) ~ 1)
  
#   Function to evaluate estimate of censuring probability
  Ghat <- function(t) {
    sapply(t, function(x) {
      idx <- max(which(km_cens$time <= x))
      if (length(idx) == 0) return(1)
      km_cens$surv[idx]
    })
  }
  
  brier_one_time <- function(t) {
    
#    Saving all rows belonging to horizon t
    sub <- merged[merged$horizon == t, ]
#    Saving the observed survival status
    status_t <- as.numeric(sub$time > t)
#    Saving the predicted survival probability: 1 - pred since dynpred outputs cumulative risk (event probability) instead of survival probabilities 
    pred_t <- 1 - sub$pred

#    Calculating the IPCW weights
    w <- ifelse(sub$time > t,
                1 / Ghat(t),
                ifelse(sub$event == 1 & sub$time <= t,
                       1 / Ghat(sub$time),
                       0))
    
#     Calculating the IPCW-adjusted Brier score
    mean(w * (status_t - pred_t)^2, na.rm = TRUE)
  }
  
#   Calculating the Brier score for each horizon
  brier_scores <- sapply(times, brier_one_time)
#   Saving to output
  output$Brier <- brier_scores
  
#   Integrated Brier Score (IBS)
  IBS <- sum(diff(times) *
               (brier_scores[-1] + brier_scores[-length(brier_scores)]) / 2) /
         (max(times) - landmark)
#   Saving to output
  output$IBS <- IBS
  
  return(output)
}
```

```{r}
# Computing Brier scores for 2 class jlcmm for NfL
brier_jlcmm_m1_nfl <- brier_jlcmm(
  model = jlcmm_m1_nfl,
  data = newdata,
  ID = "RID",
  time_var = "time_years",
  surv_time_var = "time_to_event",
  event_var = "event",
  times = times,
  landmark = 1
)

# Computing Brier scores for 2 class jlcmm for ptau
brier_jlcmm_m1_ptau <- brier_jlcmm(
  model = jlcmm_m1_ptau,
  data = newdata,
  ID = "RID",
  time_var = "time_years",
  surv_time_var = "time_to_event",
  event_var = "event",
  times = times,
  landmark = 1
)

# Computing Brier scores for 3 class jlcmm for ptau
brier_jlcmm_m2_ptau <- brier_jlcmm(
  model = jlcmm_m2_ptau,
  data = newdata,
  ID = "RID",
  time_var = "time_years",
  surv_time_var = "time_to_event",
  event_var = "event",
  times = times,
  landmark = 1
)

# ON IN-SAMPLE DATA 
# Computing Brier scores for 2 class multivariate jlcmm for ptau & NfL
brier_mult_jlcmm_m1 <- brier_jlcmm(
  model = mult_jlcmm_m1,
  data = jlcmm_data,
  ID = "RID",
  time_var = "time_years",
  surv_time_var = "time_to_event",
  event_var = "event",
  times = times,
  landmark = 1
)
```

## Table of the results

```{r}
# Time-dependent Brier score for Cox and joint models summarized in a table
results_table_Brier <- data.frame(
  Time = times,

  Cox_p_tau = cox_brier$`Cox p-tau`,
  Cox_NfL = cox_brier$`Cox NfL`,
  Cox_both = cox_brier$`Cox both`,

  JM_p_tau = brier_ptau_jm,
  JM_NfL = brier_nfl_jm,
  # JM_both = brier_multi_jm,
  
  JLCMM_2_NfL = brier_jlcmm_m1_nfl$Brier,
  JLCMM_2_ptau = brier_jlcmm_m1_ptau$Brier
  # JLCMM_3_ptau = brier_jlcmm_m2_ptau$Brier
)
round(results_table_Brier, 3)
```

```{r}
# Saving the table of the Brier score results
saveRDS(results_table_Brier, "tables/results_table_Brier.rds")
```

## Plot of the results

```{r}
plot_data <- results_table_Brier %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = -Time,
    names_to = "Model",
    values_to = "Brier"
  )

# plot for the Cox models
ggplot(plot_data %>% filter(grepl("Cox", Model)),
       aes(x = Time, y = Brier, color = Model)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.5) +
  geom_point(aes(shape = Model), size = 2) +
  ylim(0, 1) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Time-dependent Brier Score for Cox Models",
    x = "Time",
    y = "Brier score"
  )

# plot for the joint models
ggplot(plot_data %>% filter(grepl("J", Model)),
       aes(x = Time, y = Brier, color = Model)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.5) +
  geom_point(aes(shape = Model), size = 2) +
  ylim(0, 1) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Time-dependent Brier Score for Joint Models",
    x = "Time",
    y = "Brier score"
  )
```

```{r}
# Saving plots of the Brier score results
png("figures/Brier_plot_cox.png", width = 400, height = 300)
ggplot(plot_data %>% filter(grepl("Cox", Model)),
       aes(x = Time, y = Brier, color = Model)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.5) +
  geom_point(aes(shape = Model), size = 2) +
  ylim(0, 1) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Time-dependent Brier Score for Cox Models",
    x = "Time",
    y = "Brier score"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

dev.off()

png("figures/Brier_plot_jm.png", width = 400, height = 300)
ggplot(plot_data %>% filter(grepl("J", Model)),
       aes(x = Time, y = Brier, color = Model)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.5) +
  geom_point(aes(shape = Model), size = 2) +
  ylim(0, 1) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Time-dependent Brier Score for Joint Models",
    x = "Time",
    y = "Brier score"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

dev.off()
```
